name: shared-core
description: Language-agnostic orchestration, meta-skills, governance. Any language adapter depends on this.

agents.name: sdk-semver-devil
agents.reason: prompt body cites Go module semver conventions; role itself is language-neutral

agents.name: sdk-design-devil
agents.reason: examples reference Go API shape; review heuristics generalize

agents.name: sdk-security-devil
agents.reason: TLS / credential checks use Go-idiomatic code samples; threat model is generic

agents.name: sdk-overengineering-critic
agents.reason: examples reference Go idioms (unused fields, premature abstraction); critique applies to any OO-ish language

skills.name: tdd-patterns
skills.reason: examples use Go `*_test.go` and `testing.T`; TDD cycle itself is language-neutral

skills.name: idempotent-retry-safety
skills.reason: code snippets use Go context + errgroup; retry classification generalizes

generalization_debt.resolution_policy: Flagged here so the v0.5.0 second-language pilot knows the backlog. No action required in v0.4.0. When a second language adapter is authored, each item gets either (a) a language-neutral rewrite of the prompt/body, (b) move to the go package if role turns out to also be Go-specific, or (c) a split into shared + per-language parts.

notes.pass_scope: v0.4.0 scaffolding — structure only. No runtime consumer reads this file yet. Dispatch refactor deferred to a later pass.
notes.contains_drift_gates: G06, G90 (strict), G116 ship in shared-core because drift-prevention is language-neutral and is load-bearing for every run. Removing them from shared-core would let intake proceed on a drifted repo.
notes.contains_meta_baselines: G81 (baseline-advance), G83 (determinism), G84 (output-shape hash), G85 (learning-notifications), G86 (quality regression) are cross-run observability gates; language-neutral.
notes.G04_mcp_health: WARN-only by design (rule 31). Shared-core because MCP fallback policy applies to any language.
